summary:
Given the root of a binary tree, return the largest possible sum of values along any path.

A path here is any sequence of connected nodes where each consecutive pair shares an edge; a node may appear at most once and the path need not touch the root. You want the path whose node values add up to the maximum total.

examples.explanation: The path 2 -> 1 -> 3 sums to 6, the highest achievable.
The path 15 -> 20 -> 7 sums to 42; including the negative root would only lower the total.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 3 * 10^4].
A valid path contains at least one node and may start and end at any nodes.
